title: Resolve CHAR410 Signatures Before Expiration
approves: Andrew and required signer before submission
due: 2026-07-14 expiration warning; review daily until resolved
tool: NY Charities portal + saved filing packet + DSG GPT
deliverable: Required CHAR410 signature or correction path is confirmed, saved, and ready before the July 14 expiration warning.
saveFinalIn: DSG Share Folder > Finance and Compliance
context: This is the sharpest clock in the Hub. Do not let broader cleanup or planning displace the filing/signature path.
nextSteps: Open the saved CHAR410 packet or NY Charities portal status.
Identify the exact missing signature, correction, or submission blocker.
Route only the necessary signer/correction request to the correct person.
Save confirmation, receipt, screenshot, or blocker note in the compliance folder.
Review daily until signature/submission state is confirmed before July 14.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a CHAR410 signature/correction tracker. Include the July 14 expiration warning, required signer/correction path, daily review rule, proof to save, owner/support/approver, and a short blocker note format. Keep it procedural and do not invent filing facts.

title: Set Read AI Meeting-Capture Policy Before Connecting Calendar
support: Karena / Omari if team norms are needed
due: Before connecting Read AI to calendar or meetings
tool: Read AI settings + Google Calendar + DSG GPT
deliverable: A written policy says whether Read AI may auto-join, when manual capture is allowed, how consent is handled, and where meeting summaries can be stored.
context: Calendar connection should wait until privacy, consent, and auto-join behavior are understood. Default to manual/no-auto-join unless Andrew explicitly approves otherwise.
nextSteps: Review Read AI account, calendar, and auto-join settings before connecting anything new.
Default to manual capture only until Andrew approves an auto-join rule.
Define consent language for internal and external meetings.
Decide where summaries, transcripts, and action items can be stored.
Document the final rule before using it in team meetings.
prompt: In DSG GPT, draft a practical Read AI meeting-capture policy for DSG. Include manual vs auto-join default, consent language, internal vs external meeting rules, storage rules for transcripts/summaries, owner/approver, and a setup checklist before connecting Google Calendar.

title: Finish Narrow Participant Follow-Ups, Zoom Details, and Logging
due: Immediate; keep PII out of the Hub
deliverable: Known participant follow-ups are sent or scheduled, Zoom details are correct, responses are logged safely, and no PII is exposed in team-facing Hub notes.
saveFinalIn: DSG Share Folder > Programming > Workshop Email Communication
context: The broad registrant-care item is considered complete, but narrower follow-ups still need controlled handling and logging.
nextSteps: Use Zeffy/email records to confirm only the remaining participant follow-ups.
Confirm Zoom details before sending any scheduling note.
Send or schedule only approved follow-up messages.
Log response status and next action in the safe records location.
Do not copy participant PII, payment details, or private replies into the Hub.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a PII-safe participant follow-up tracker for remaining July workshop follow-ups. Include Zoom details, sent/scheduled status, response logging, owner/support/approver, what not to put in team-facing notes, and a short final-proof checklist.

title: Verify Public Workshop Copy Is Current and Save Proof
support: Marketing support / Omari for Facebook Page
deliverable: Website, social, listing, and event surfaces no longer promote unavailable July workshop programming; screenshots or notes prove final state.
context: Public cleanup remains active until proof exists. Do not mark website/social/listing work complete from memory or assumptions.
nextSteps: Check the DSG website for stale July 6-11 registration or availability language.
Check Buffer, Instagram, Facebook Page, Eventbrite, and community listings for stale promotion.
Replace or remove unavailable registration language with current-status or future-interest language only after Andrew approval.
Save screenshots or final-state notes for each checked surface.
Do not announce future dates until the post-mortem, tracking, budget, and offer reset are approved.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a public-surface cleanup checklist for DSG. Cover website, Buffer, Instagram, Facebook Page, Eventbrite, and listings. Include replacement wording guardrails, proof to save, approval rules, and a no-future-dates rule until post-mortem decisions are complete.

title: Confirm Ads Stopped and Archive Spend Evidence
support: Clark / marketing support if screenshots are needed
approves: Clark for finance records; Andrew for platform status
tool: Meta / Google ad dashboards + Relay + Google Drive
deliverable: Campaigns are confirmed off with platform proof/export, final spend is saved, and Facebook receipt reminders are handled in Relay.
context: This proof-sensitive task must stay visible until screenshot/export and receipt handling evidence exists.
nextSteps: Confirm all relevant campaigns are off in the ad platform dashboards.
Save screenshot or export showing final status and spend.
Upload or forward Facebook receipts to Relay as needed.
Capture learning notes for the workshop post-mortem.
Do not treat ad-stop status as verified until proof is saved.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create an ad-spend evidence checklist for DSG. Include ad-stop proof, screenshot/export requirements, final spend, Relay receipt handling, owner/support/approver, save location, and what should be summarized for the post-mortem.

title: Record Zettle POS Equipment and Payment Admin Status
approves: Clark for finance records; Andrew for account/admin changes
deliverable: Zettle/POS equipment, account access, payment routing, and finance-record status are documented without exposing credentials.
context: POS/payment records are part of the current finance cleanup and should be visible separately from Zeffy reconciliation.
nextSteps: Inventory known Zettle/POS equipment and ownership/custody status.
Confirm account access and payment routing without sharing credentials in the Hub.
Check whether any PayPal/Zettle records need export or finance filing.
Save receipt/export/account-status notes in the Finance folder.
Escalate any access, recovery, or bank-routing changes to Andrew and Clark before acting.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a Zettle/POS finance-record checklist for DSG. Include equipment inventory, account access, payment routing, export/receipt needs, credential safety, owner/support/approver, save location, and blockers to escalate.

title: Reconcile Zeffy, Relay, Refund Exposure, and Payment Records
approves: Clark for finance records; Andrew for cancellation policy
tool: Zeffy + Relay + Google Drive + DSG GPT
deliverable: Zeffy revenue/payouts are matched to Relay deposits, refund/transfer/1-on-1 exposure is documented safely, and payment-record blockers are listed.
context: Financial cleanup needs records-level proof, not just narrative status. Keep participant-specific payment details out of the Hub.
nextSteps: Compare Zeffy reports with Relay deposits and finance records.
Document refund, transfer, or 1-on-1 exposure without participant PII.
Identify any mismatch, missing receipt, or payment-account blocker.
Save finance notes, exports, and receipts in the Finance folder.
Bring policy questions to Andrew before making participant-facing commitments.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a Zeffy/Relay reconciliation checklist. Include revenue, payouts, deposits, refund/transfer exposure, privacy limits, owner/support/approver, save location, and blocker note format.

title: Verify Google Admin Security and Save Minimal Status Proof
support: Trusted admin support only if needed
tool: Google Account security activity + Google Workspace Admin
deliverable: Google admin/security alert or account-access concern is verified as expected or escalated, with only minimal resolved/escalated status recorded.
context: This task must stay visible until direct proof exists. Keep private security details, devices, codes, recovery data, and credentials out of the Hub.
nextSteps: Check Google account activity directly from the admin account.
Confirm whether recent sign-in or access activity was expected.
If unexpected, secure the account before continuing admin work.
Record only resolved/escalated status and date, not sensitive security details.
Do not delegate credential or recovery-setting changes casually.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a brief admin security check note for DSG. Include how to verify a Google sign-in or access alert, what to record if expected, what to do if unexpected, and what details must stay out of public or team-facing notes.

title: Prepare August/September Focus Meeting From Omari Input
approves: Andrew; board only for governance or budget commitments
tool: Google Calendar + DSG GPT + Google Docs
deliverable: A focused meeting agenda defines what August/September should accomplish, what is not worth re-litigating, and what decisions need owner/support/approval clarity.
context: Use the logic that programming and audience must work together: programming helps define the audience, and audience reality should shape delivery, structure, and operations.
nextSteps: Use Omari-informed context to frame August/September priorities.
Keep July workshop debrief focused on what changes future operating decisions, not blame or stale process details.
Name concrete meeting outputs: decisions, owners, next steps, and what gets paused.
Separate strategic focus from detailed post-mortem cleanup.
Save the final agenda and decisions in the leadership/strategy folder.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create an August/September focus meeting agenda for DSG. The meeting should decide what programming and audience work should accomplish together, what operations must change, what not to re-litigate from July Workshop Week, and what owners/approvers are needed. Keep it practical and meeting-output focused.

title: Schedule Separate Workshop Post-Mortem and Funnel Reset
approves: Andrew for meeting plan; board for budget or strategy commitments
tool: Google Calendar + DSG GPT + Google Docs/Sheets
deliverable: A separate post-mortem is scheduled to review workshop performance, funnel lessons, website/tracking needs, budget/expert options, and next-cycle decisions.
context: Do not overload the August/September focus meeting with every workshop detail. Use this separate post-mortem for metrics and funnel reset.
nextSteps: Schedule the post-mortem after immediate public and participant cleanup is stable.
Collect registration, social, listing, ad, and website data.
Identify what changed, what failed, and what should not be repeated.
Review whether outside marketing/platform support is needed.
Capture decisions before future dates or offers are announced.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a July workshop post-mortem agenda. Include metrics to collect, funnel review, social/listing review, ad-spend proof, audience engagement questions, website/tracking needs, expert-cost options, decisions needed before future dates, and a clean decision log section.

title: Prepare Andrew/Richard Role-Clarity Conversation
approves: Andrew; board only if governance changes are proposed
tool: Google Calendar + DSG GPT + Google Docs
deliverable: Andrew and Richard have a focused conversation on role lanes, decision rights, recurring responsibilities, and what should stop routing through Andrew by default.
context: This should be separate from the workshop post-mortem and from broad August/September programming decisions.
nextSteps: Define what Andrew needs from Richard before the conversation.
List recurring responsibilities and decision lanes that are currently unclear.
Identify which decisions Richard can make, recommend, or must escalate.
Document only role/process decisions, not sensitive interpersonal commentary.
Turn decisions into operating rules or follow-up tasks.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create an Andrew/Richard role-clarity conversation agenda for DSG. Include decision lanes, recurring responsibilities, escalation rules, what Andrew should stop owning by default, what should be documented, and a concise follow-up format.

title: Check Hub, FormSubmit, and GitHub Pages Reliability
deliverable: Hub loads current data, task suggestions and review submissions email Andrew, cache/versioning is updated, and completion behavior does not hide tasks before evidence review.
context: The Hub itself needs a reliability check after data and completion-flow changes.
nextSteps: Open the public Hub and confirm the latest task data loads.
Submit a harmless test suggestion or review signal if appropriate.
Confirm FormSubmit delivery reaches Andrew or the intended mailbox.
Confirm clicking task-review submission does not remove the task from shared or local view.
Save a short reliability note with date, result, and blocker if any.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a Hub reliability test checklist for DSG. Include GitHub Pages data freshness, cache-bust/version check, FormSubmit delivery, review-signal behavior, completion visibility rules, owner/approver, and a short test-result note.

title: Capture Team Replies, Edits, and Blockers
support: Team / Omari / team leads if delegated
deliverable: Team replies, edits, blockers, assignment questions, and decision needs are captured in a simple follow-up note only when evidence supports the change.
context: The active work is feedback and blocker capture, not recreating another broad pre-send brief.
nextSteps: Review replies to the Team Next Steps email.
Capture blockers, assignment edits, and questions.
Update assignments only with source-backed evidence.
Do not store raw private notes or unnecessary personal details.
Bring unresolved decisions to the next team call.
prompt: In DSG GPT, help me turn team replies into a clean blocker-capture note. Include what to log, what not to log, evidence rules, due timing, and a short format for blockers, assignment changes, and decisions needed before the next team call.

title: Stage Funding Readiness With Approval Gates
support: Clark / Omari / Vaughn if guidance call moves forward
approves: Board for sensitive identity or sponsor-benefit language
due: CitizensNYC deadline July 27; keep behind urgent cleanup
tool: Google Docs + Google Sheets + Grants Folder + DSG GPT
deliverable: IRS letter, budget, program description, impact metrics, equity narrative, concept note, CitizensNYC/NYCT fit notes, and approval flags are staged but not sent externally without approval.
context: Funding prep should continue quietly but must not displace urgent compliance, public cleanup, participant follow-up, or finance proof.
nextSteps: Stage IRS letter, budget, program description, and metrics.
Prepare equity narrative and concept note as internal drafts.
Prepare CitizensNYC and NYCT fit notes.
Flag identity language, fiscal claims, and sponsor benefits for approval before external use.
Keep this behind urgent cleanup and proof-sensitive tasks.
prompt: In DSG GPT, create a funding readiness brief for DSG that stays below urgent cleanup. Include staged materials, CitizensNYC July 27 note, NYCT fit notes, owner/support/approver, sensitive-language review, save location, and a clear rule not to use identity or sponsor-benefit language externally without approval.
